content:
# Top 5 posts from r/MovieSuggestions day

## 1. Erotic films - non violent, hot, good cinematography

**Score:** 101
**Body:** My partner and I are looking for movies to get us in the mood, but it seems like we have a bit of a particular taste.

We don't mind tension, but it has to actually lead somewhere. Last night we saw Portrait of a Lady on Fire and while the film itself was interesting, it missed actual hot scenes.

We don't like anything violent. It seems like a lot of films focus on taboo like incest, non-consent, "she wants it, but she doesn't want it." Also no very young actresses. "Blue is the warmest colour" seems like a top pick, but as soon as my gf reads "15 year old" she gets turned off.

edit : She also doesn't like affairs much. Someone falling in love with someone else and leaving their partner is fine. People lying to their partner is a big turnoff!

Sexwise we'd really prefer for it to be an exploration of love and sex! Healthy relationships, communication, good sex.

Some story would be great, but I guess mood pieces - artsy films could also work. We don't mind weird stuff, some surrealism. As long as it kinda goes somewhere? Humour is great. Thrillers are also fine. Romance is also fine

We don't mind graphic stuff - both male and female. As long as the scene is hot, the actors don't have to be top models. Real people are fine.

Good cinematography. Nature shots would be a good addition.

### Top 9 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Rochelle Rochelle - a young girl’s strange, erotic journey from Milan to Minsk. 
2. Short Bus - really inventive film which uses actual (but not at all porn-y) sex to explore the ins and outs of having a healthy relationship with yourself and others.  Great humour, compelling characters and a heartwarming story!
3. All Tinto Brass films. Monamour especially
4. You could try 9 songs which is basically just pretentious porn. It’s not very good as a film but I think it fits. 
  - When it comes down to it, I think pretentious porn is what we're after, hah
5. 1977	Bilitis

1974	Emmanuelle

1975	Emmanuelle: L'antivierge (Emmanuelle 2)

1977	Suor Emanuelle

1976	Black Cobra Woman

1980	 La muchacha de las bragas de oro-Girl with the Golden Panties

1981	Divine Emanuelle

1983	Gabriela, Cravo e Canela

1985	Silip

1991	La belle noiseuse

1991	Paprika

1996	Kama Sutra: A Tale of Love

1999	Pantaleón y las Visitadoras

2000	Tra(sgre)dire/Cheeky

2003	A Good Lawyer's Wife

2005	The Civilization of Maxwell Bright

2007	California Dreamin'

2007	Lust, Caution

2008	A Tale of Legendary Libido (Garoojigi)

2010	Natalie

2011	Madrid, 1987

2011	Now and Later

2011	Whore’s Glory

2012	A Muse (Eungyo)

2012	B.A. Pass

2012	Due West: Our Sex Journey

2013	Nymphomaniac

2016	The Handmaiden

2011	Love Actually... Sucks!

2005	Batalla en el cielo

2001	Samsara

1981	Lady Chatterly’s Lover

1972	Le Grand Blond Avec Une Chaussure Noire
  - This guy erotic films.
6. My Awkward Sexual Adventure (2012) is a Canadian romantic comedy about an uptight accountant, Jordan Abrams, who hires an exotic dancer, Julia, to teach him how to be a better lover after his girlfriend dumps him for being "terrible in bed". The film follows his journey as he navigates strip clubs, massage parlors, and S&M to gain experience and win back his ex.
7. Sex and Lucia (2001)
8. Short Bus

## 2. Arrival - Movies that made you feel the same way...

**Score:** 35
**Body:** Arrival is one of my favorite movies. Without spoiling, I would love to know others suggestions for similar vibe/storytelling/emotions. Even if not in sci-fi space. Originality, suspense, payoff. 

### Top 9 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. There's a bunch that I think got what you're asking for. 

Interstellar, Annihilation, Moon. Maybe Blade Runner 2049
  - Second “Moon.”  Sam Rockwell is very good in it. 
    - Agreed. I like him as an actor
2. Gattaca
3. sunshine :)
4. Contact (1997)

Another Earth (2011)

Inception (2010)

Sphere (1998)

Stargate (1994) - less for the feel, more for deciphering another language. An adventure romp.
5. Interstellar. Contact. Predestination. Aniara. I also love Arrival btw.  
6. Annihilation. Its a bit more suspenseful/graphic but it it shares a lot of characteristics as arrival
7. Project Hail Mary
8. Contact

## 3. Looking for feminist movies

**Score:** 18
**Body:** Please no spoilers, I like to go in blind.

I’ll watch suggestions in any language or made at any time. 

Thank you to anyone who suggests a movie. 

### Top 10 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. The Craft

Barbie

Norma Rae

Party Girl

Jennifer's Body

Set It Off

Hidden Figures

Agora

Wonder Woman

Now and Then

Waiting to Exhale

What's Love Got to Do With It

Persepolis. from the brilliant graphic novel






2. Orlando 
(it is a feminist masterpiece, directed by a woman and based on feminist hero Virginia Woolf book)
3. Whip It
4. The Legend of Billie Jean
5. Thelma and Louise


6. My Brilliant Career (1979)

9 to 5 (1980)

A League of Their Own (1992)

The Associate (1996)
7. On the Basis of Sex
8. Klute. Jane Fonda ensured her character was not a one dimensional prostitute.  The character had intelligence, street smarts, and goals. Also the movie showed how there are men who prey on vulnerable women because they can get away with it.  Prostitutes and drug addicts are sometimes deemed to have deserved bad outcomes. 
9. Whale Rider
10. Promising Young Woman (2020)

Revenge (2017)

But I'm a Cheerleader (1999)

Le Bonheur (1965)

The Passion of Joan of Arc (1928)

## 4. movies structured like a video game

**Score:** 14
**Body:** something like kill bill, scott pilgrim, godzilla x kong where the protagonist has to go through stages, beat bosses at the end of them, get new items and skills along the way and end up fighting the final boss

### Top 10 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Hardcore Henry (2015)
2. The Raid
3. It’s right there in the title - Boss Level (2020)
  - Good movie. I also think of it as a time loop movie due it's structure.
4. Free Guy
5. Ready Player One
6. Hundreds of Beavers 
7. Boy Kills World 
8. *Overlord*, 2018
9. Edge of Tomorrow 

## 5. Movies like Pride and Prejudice, and Sense and Sensibility

**Score:** 14
**Body:** please recommend movies that are quite similar to these two 🥲 I cannot fathom how much I love these films. I would absolutely love recommendations with the same kind of longing, beautiful cinematography, meaningful dialogue, and quiet romance that leaves you thinking about it for days afterward 🥲

### Top 10 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Ryan's Daughter 

The Go-between 

A Room with a View

Howard's End

A Passage to India
2. Remains of the Day. Emma, 
3. My list has some repeats from others' recommendations, but here are some of my favorites along these lines:

A Room with a View  
The Age of Innocence  
Jane Eyre (2011)  
The Remains of the Day  
Emma (both the 1996 and 2020 versions)  
Far From the Madding Crowd  
The Taste of Things  
The Go-Between  
Little Women (both the 1994 and 2019 versions)  
Howard's End  
Ever After
4. Lady Chatterley’s Lover - warning - it’s very steamy 

I’m sure you’ve seen this one but just in case - Ever After

And here’s a left turn because it’s not a period piece at all and a series, but it leaves you with all the emotions you described - One Day on Netflix with Leo Woodall
5. Somewhere in time.  
6. Heat and Dust

Remains of the Day 

(Any of the Merchant and Ivory productions)

Atonement 

7. Barry Lyndon (1975) 
8. Little Women (1994)
9. Shakespeare in Love 
10. North and South (2004 miniseries) has a lot of longing and class conflict.

one of my favorite period dramas that has a lot of forbidden love and longing stares is A Royal Affair (2012) with Alicia vikander and Mads Mikkelsen. It’s a danish movie about the queen of Denmark and her falling in love with the kings doctor.

There’s a whole slow motion dance sequence that I’m pretty sure was directly ripped off from the 2005 pride and prejudice movie.

# Top 5 posts from r/moviecritic day

## 1. who’s the most talented actress here?

**Score:** 675

### Top 7 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. when wolf of wall street came out I thought: oh that's the lady from My name's Earl 
  - I’m learning that Margot Robbie and this Samara Weaving person are not both Margot Robbie right now for the first time. I’m not joking 😔
2. Seriously thought the first 3 were just Margot Robbie in different roles before I saw the names. 
  - There’s a version of this meme where the 4th photo is another photo of Margot Robbie with a different name 
3. Probably Robbie but I prefer Weaving's filmography 
4. Younger me did not appreciate how actual good looking Jamie Presley is, also I love her in my name is earl.
  - She’s classic in Not another teen movie 
    - We’re black, we know it, we shake our big bootys and show it. 

We’re not white, we’re not white, we’re definitly now white
5. Jamie Presley all day please 
6. I, Tanya sold me on Robbie's skill.

## 2. What’s everyone’s opinion on Arrival? I watched it for the first time.

**Score:** 641

### Top 7 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. It's a melancholic story that's told very well. The twist is profoundly sad and worthy of debate. 
2. I coincidentally watched it yesterday. I saw it in the theater when it came out 10 years ago but it was on TV randomly yesterday. I had completely forgotten how good it is
3. Well since you watched it for the first time, what is your take on it, 007?
  - A masterpiece. It’s a 10/10
    - Yes, it was quite good.
    - Now, watch it again. Hits totally differently. 
4. I only saw it once but I remember really liking the aliens design and their approach to language. It wasn’t just humanoid, bipedal biology but something truly foreign.
5. It was the first time in a very long time I felt a movie had simply assumed that I was smart. I really liked that about it
  - Yes, it was wrong, but I still liked it.
6. Awesome prequel to man of steel

## 3. What's the most overhyped movie of the last 5 years?

**Score:** 352
**Body:** Not necessarily a bad movie. Just one where the hype was so big that when you finally watched it you were like "...that's it?" Reviews were glowing, everyone was talking about it, social media wouldn't shut up about it, and then you actually sat down and watched it and it just didn't live up to any of it. What's that movie for you?

### Top 6 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Wicked.  Not even close imo.
  - I fell asleep in both of them. Yawning just thinking about them,
    - My wife loves these movies.  Dragged me along to both of them, and I was dreading that second one.   They’re like melatonin in movie form.
2. The Avatar movies that somehow make tons of money even though no one ever seems to watch or talk about them.
3. Going crazy for Oppenheimer, and bragging about it was "it was filmed for IMAX', why? So you could watch talking heads in IMAX?
  - It felt like a three hour montage
4. Tenet.

Partially because the movie is just ok, but mainly because this is the film that Nolan insisted on releasing mid-pandemic.  For months he made this seem like his magnum opus, then everyone saw it and wondered why it couldn’t have waited a few months.
  - My main problem with Tenet is if you saw it in theaters you couldn’t hear what people were saying and if you watch it at home you most certainly need subtitles 
  - Or a few years. So they could do some rewrites and reshoots and make it…. IDK… coherent?
5. I wouldn't even call Oppenheimer hype, that was just good PR and advertising.

## 4. Cameron wanting to cut Avatar costs proves even he knows the sequels are too much

**Score:** 282
**Body:** Let's be real, Avatar 2 made bank, but did it have the same cultural impact as the first? Not even close. Now Cameron's talking about slashing costs and time on 4 and 5? Sounds like even he's feeling the pressure. He's probably realizing that audiences aren't as hyped for blue cat people as Disney wants them to be, and he needs to find a way to wrap this up without bankrupting the studio. The sequels need to justify their existence, not just bleed money.

### Top 4 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. They dont need to be made.


  - You don’t want to watch the same story for a fourth and fifth time? 
  - I'm not sure even Avatar 1 had a huge cultural impact. It made a lot of money but didn't seem to leave deep footprints.
2. That's a pretty crappy article, riddled with errors:


"The Disney franchise has now earned around $987 million."


The first movie made that in like its first month
3. I've also wondered how these films, for being some of the highest grossing films in cinema history, seem to have so vanishingly little cultural impact. I've never met a single avatar fan, had a single conversation about avatar. Never seen anyone wear any avatar merch. 
  - I love every second of watching them and then I don’t think about them at all until another releases lol
  - the lasting cultural impact of avatar is people constantly saying it has no cultural impact. 
  - It's more a franchise for adults that like movies, as opposed to just being merch advertisements like Star Wars and Marvel. I don't see anyone wearing Oppenheimer merch either, and that made a billion.
  - \- Some things are simply for mass consumption rather than lasting impact. 

\- Nobody’s surprised that some random Mcdonalds franchisee with 8 locations makes much more money than the most celebrated Michelin-starred restaurants out there. 

\- Mobile gaming dwarfs the combined size of console and PC gaming. 

\- Faith-based media, Spanish language TV, Tyler Perry movies… all of them make fucking bank but outside of very specific niche audiences literally nobody cares or knows anything about them. 

\- Thomas Kinkade was probably the richest and most ubiquitous artist in America. 

\- Garth Brooks sold more albums in the US than the Beatles.  

\- James Patterson and Danielle Steel in literature, 

\- NCIS which was the most watched drama for like a decade… 

all of the above with miniscule cultural footprint compared to what one could have expected. 
  - Because they are having a greater impact on technology. The mo cap Cameron has been doing has been pretty game changing for the industry. The Avatar movies are basically expensive science projects. He made 2 and 3 with a prototype camera that was in the experimental phase. Cameron is a mad scientist first and a director 2nd. 

## 5. What movie wasn't what you were expecting based on marketing?

**Score:** 261
**Body:** Doesn't have to mean you hated it, just that it wasn't what you thought it was going to be. I like the movie Bridesmaids, but I thought it was going to be more about the bridesmaids, the process of them becoming and being bridesmaids, and that the other bridesmaids were going to get more screen time and focus. Instead, it focused on Annie's experience and her and Lillian's relationship. I laughed a lot and did not dislike the movie, but it just wasn't what I was expecting it to be. "Bridesmaids" just implied to me that it was going to be more about being bridesmaids and that the others were going to have a bigger role/part. 

### Top 8 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Downsizing. FML 🙃
  - I am still pissed about that years later. I want the fun comedy promised!
2. Click
  - Left the theater SOBBING after this movi and cried the whole way home. It really did a number on me, man.
    - Lmaooo, I saw it on a second date when I was 17. I was a late bloomer and my date was THE guy in the alt scene in my city. He looked like Elvis but taller. Just gorgeous. I was in shock when he asked me out. 

…he was also stupid and had the emotional intelligence of a teaspoon. He was NOT into me sobbing over the Adam Sandler movie. 

3. Both “Cable Guy” and “Truman Show”.  I was of an age where Jim Carrey was the rubber faced Ace Ventura guy.  Both of those movies were against type for him.  I was disappointed as a kid, but now see their value.
4. In Bruges.

The advertising made it out to be a slightly dark comedy, instantly forgettable.

Watching it... So much better, and so much darker. A redemption story for people who can't be redeemed. Far better than I was expecting, and three great characters that try to do what they thought was the right thing.
5. Spring Breakers

Loved the movie but was not expecting Salena Gomez to be in like 10 minutes of the movie based on the commercials.
6. Pan's Labyrinth. Went into it with a friend at age 16 expecting some kind of Tim-Burton-like fantasy movie.
7. Burn After Reading. I don't really know what I was expecting....but it wasn't that.

---

# Top 5 posts from r/TrueFilm day

## 1. How Do Novel Releases and Film Adaptations Work?

**Score:** 2
**Body:** Stephen King released the novel; Christine in April 1983.  
Then Carpenter directs and releases the film adaptation in December the same year. Did King option out the novel it be made into a script, or did someone read it in April, write a screenplay in a week, then get production behind it, and release it in 7 months?!😅  
I legitimately don’t understand how that works.

Also! What film adaptations close to the novel release are your favorites?!

### Top 4 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. If a book already has buzz behind it before it gets released, either because the author is super-popular or because the industry is excited for their own reasons, it can get optioned well before it hits the shelves.

Lately, there have been cases where a book gets optioned before release because of booktok buzz, but then it gets absolutely trashed when it comes out, and the idea of a movie adapation is just never mentioned again.
  - I thought that must be the only way. I just never recalled that process specifically. Thanks for the clarification!😄 
It came to mind because I recently read The Road and was watching the movie for the first time (somehow I just avoided seeing it even though I knew I’d like it) . But that released only 3 years after the novel. When I read the novel I swore it read like something from 1940’s or 50’s. 
    - I think “securing the rights” is relatively cheap, and the authors probably have it in the contract they get more once the movie starts production and then even more if it’s a hit. I’d also suspect they’ve become smart enough to put clauses like “if the rights holder does not start production on an adaptation within (some amount of time) the rights revert back to author” and can be shopped around again. Just guesses, but I do a lot of contracts in my work and those are two things I’d insist on.
      - Yes — purchasing the rights for a studio is chump change. The purchase price is considerably more but doesnt get paid unless or until the movie is “green lit” (ie enters production, a fun sounding mystery of a step that might be filming or casting or building sets or whenever.)
    - you're also thinking early 80s stephen king, very few authors have ever been on that level. The only equivalent I'd say is JK post harry potter
2. A number of Michael Crichton novels were optioned as films before they were even written.

If your books sell really well and the films adapted from them have also been huge hits, then eventually studios want the rights to your work before it's even finished. It feels like a sure thing, they  get the rights before anyone else can make an offer, and the buzz  behind the book helps the film.

King was definitely an author on that kind of hot streak in '83. Anything new he released was likely going to get a film made of it. After the success of the adaptations of *Carrie*, *'Salem's Lot*, and *The Shining*, the race was on. In 1983 alone adaptations of *The Dead Zone*, *Cujo*, and *Christine* were all released and *Firestarter* was still in production eventually coming out in May 1984. That was pretty much all the novels he had published up until that point. The only two left out were those published under his Richard Bachman pseudonym, *The Stand*, and *The Dark Tower: The Gunslinger*. The Bachman books didn't have the same recognition just yet and the latter two were... not ideal for filming. So producers clearly wanted to grab whatever was available.

But while that shows a clear trend, the full story is right there on the [film's Wikipedia page](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christine_\(1983_film\)#Production):

> Producer Richard Kobritz had previously produced the 1979 miniseries Salem's Lot, also based on a Stephen King novel. Through producing the miniseries, Kobritz became acquainted with King, who sent him manuscripts of two of his novels, *Cujo*, and *Christine*. Kobritz purchased the rights to *Christine* after finding himself attracted to the novel's "celebration of America's obsession with the motorcar."

And:

> Filming began in April 1983, mere days after the King novel had been published

So that's it. The producer had worked with King already and bought the rights while it was still unpublished. Presumably pretty far in advance since the novel *Cujo* came out in 1981.
3. I used to be a scriptreader for different production companies—basically I was the first one to read something coming in from an agent and I’d tell more highly paid people if it was worth their time to read it themselves—and I would frequently get typed manuscripts of books. The time between an author finishing a book and it hitting shelves can easily be over a year.
4. In modern days, if a novel is sufficiently buzzy, the film rights might go up for auction with the publication rights. 

Back then, it was similar-ish. Mostly via connections though.

Think Jaws. There the movie was already in production before the book came out because one of the producer's wives was an editor for Cosmopolitan, and she got a review of the book to edit that mentioned it might make for a good movie adaption.

## 2. Mortal Kombat 2 Review

**Score:** 0
**Body:** The battle between Earth Realm and Out World continues in Mortal Kombat 2 with more characters, more world building, more humor and of course more bloody goodness. Whereas the first Mortal Kombat back in 2021 served as a stepping stone into this film adaptation based on the iconic video game franchise the sequel clearly surpasses it and is honestly the superior film. It is entertaining as hell and offers so much more for the die hard fans. 

Karl Urban as Johnny Cage is nothing short of amazing, perfect casting for a character who plays a bitter old washed up action movie star who feels like his glory days have long passed, until destiny  gives him a new purpose by choosing him alongside many of the other Earth Realm champions to partake in Mortal Kombat in order to save earth from being conquered. 

Another actor who steals the show is Martyn Ford as Shao Kahn, a brutal, merciless, tyrant whose objective is to conquer earth and to be worshipped as a “God” while killing those who go against him. Kahn is intimidating and just an evil villain in the film overall. Excellent custom design and makeup Ford absolutely nailed playing this role. 

CJ Bloomfield as Baraka is scary as fuck at first but quickly shifts in his tone and personality (NO SPOILERS) but that fight sequence between him and Cage was over the top and hilarious and both of their chemistry on screen is comedic gold! Myself and my buddy Dylan hope to see Baraka in Mortal Kombat 3. 

Lastly, as far as characters go we have to mention Adeline Rudolph as Kitana, very mesmerizing and easy on the eyes during her scenes but also a kickass badass heroine she’s a treat on screen! There is more to Kitana in the beginning of the film but we would hate to spoil it if you’re an MK fan go see this for yourselves on the big screen. 

In regards to the fight choreography and fatalities they are completed with love and care paying tribute to the video games! They are twice as brutal, twice as creative and twice as emotional. A lot of notable characters meet their fate in Mortal Kombat 2! It’s just a rollercoaster of mixed emotions that spectators will endure but in a good way! It’s pure fun and it’s a video game adapted film sequel done 100% right! If you grew up with video games across many decades of gaming you know what to expect from this. However, if for some reason you’ve been living under a rock and don’t understand these movies then you’re not the target audience and that’s totally okay. 

The various set designs in Mortal Kombat 2 are best described as spectacular homages that are very much recognizable stages for you to choose where you wanted to fight in the games, the details of these locations where the design was also made with love and care, and would make fans both happy and nostalgic. It’s really great to see a fresh new start to Mortal Kombat on the big screen unlike its predecessors from the 90’s which were severely lacking and didn’t quite deliver the goods. Oh Did we forget to mention how horrid the VFX looked on Reptile in the original Mortal Kombat film? We rest our case. 

Myself and Dylan are looking forward to seeing what else can be accomplished in Mortal Kombat 3 and believe that the newly upgraded Mortal Kombat films have a rightful seat at the table, for one of the best video game movies of the current generation like Sonic, Mario, and potentially others.. Mortal Kombat 2 “Finishes” with an A. 

### Top 1 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Now I saw the movie so I know why this is but it’s incredibly telling that you’ve written a glowing 8-paragraph review for a movie without talking about the story even once. I appreciate your opinion but this is just really flat writing, there’s no substance here besides “it looks just like the product I expected to buy!”, it reads a lot more like you’re advertising the movie than diving into it.

If this were instead a review of a Mortal Kombat-themed night at a Universal Theme Park I don’t think you’d have to change much, is what I’m saying.
  - It’s Mortal Kombat to be fair it’s pretty self explanatory. Plus I generally don’t spoil movies I see especially since a lot of people still need to check it out. You’re correct that this is an overtly positive review and might be considered an advertisement but I got what I expected out of the film. It’s just an opinion that’s it. I’m glad you enjoyed the movie as well. 
    - To be clear, I did not enjoy the movie at all, it’s easily one of the worst things I saw all year. And I’ve played many of the games over the last 30 years. 
      - Okay sorry to hear that I personally enjoyed it. Definitely not the worst thing to come out this year but I’m not debating. 
        - Well I didn't say it was the worst thing to come out all year, it was the worst thing \*I\* saw all year. What do you think is the worst thing you've seen all year, out of curiosity?

## 3. Do all of you guys know any extras from Animal House 1978 film you guys know in person and which scenes they reveal they had appeared in?

**Score:** 0
**Body:** What do you guys think about which extras you guys know in person that appeared in Animal House 1978 film, even some of the extras that used to study at the University where they were filming. Also the locals too in that same area, you know definitely. Also share your opinions in the comment below, also talk about the person's name too or just described how they look like and what scene they appeared in. But well any suggestions about this?
